name: Aider
description: Open-source CLI pair-programmer with strong git integration

developer.comment: Created by Paul Gauthier; maintained as a community open-source project under the Aider-AI GitHub org.

first-release.comment: GitHub repository created 2023-05-09; used as the first-public-release proxy.

status.comment: Actively developed and released as of check date 2026-06-02 (~45.7k GitHub stars).

specialization.comment: Broad coding pair-programmer; not positioned for a narrower niche.

interface.comment: Primarily a terminal/CLI tool; also offers an optional browser-based web chat. Watch mode lets it work alongside any editor but it is not itself an IDE extension.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal and a browser web chat. Watch mode monitors files edited in any editor (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim) via AI comments rather than installing as a host-IDE extension.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Installed and run as a standalone CLI tool (pip/pipx/uv/Docker).

default-model-provider.comment: No single bundled default; works best with top models across providers (Gemini 2.5 Pro, Claude Sonnet, DeepSeek, OpenAI). User picks the model/provider.

model-choice.comment: Supports 15+ providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, Azure, Ollama, OpenRouter, etc.).

bring-your-own-key.comment: Pure BYOK; Aider itself has no vendor billing — users supply their own provider API key.

local-model-support.comment: Runs against local models via Ollama and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

multi-file-editing.comment: Plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files; uses a repo map for whole-codebase context.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Can chain edit -> lint/test -> fix loops (auto-lint, auto-test, /test) and architect mode plans then applies; interactive but executes multi-step tasks with limited per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: /run and /test execute shell commands; auto-test/auto-lint can run build/test as part of the loop.

mcp-support.comment: No native MCP support in core Aider as of check date; only third-party MCP wrappers/servers exist and there is an open feature request.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Runs a single linear loop. Architect mode pairs two models (planner + editor) sequentially, not parallel/spawned subagents.

codebase-indexing.comment: Builds a 'repo map' from tree-sitter symbol extraction of files rather than a semantic embeddings index; ad-hoc map, not a vector store.

planning-mode.comment: Architect/editor mode plans the solution with one model then applies edits with another; /ask gives a read-only Q&A mode.

skills.comment: Has fixed built-in slash commands but no mechanism for user-defined, reusable on-demand skills/recipes; conventions are always-on instruction files, not skills.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts images (add image files, /paste image from clipboard) for screenshot/design context.

browser-computer-use.comment: /web only scrapes a page to markdown; no ability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify changes.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational terminal edits with the user in the loop; runs multi-step edit/test loops with approval. No inline tab completion or detached async runs.

inline-completion.comment: CLI/chat tool; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs interactively in a focused terminal session; no dispatch-and-walk-away cloud/background execution.

persistent-memory.comment: No cross-session learned memory. Persistent context comes from user-maintained convention files loaded each session, not stored agent memory.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: /undo reverts the last aider-made git commit, restoring the working tree before a change; backed by its automatic git commits.

git-integration.comment: Core strength: auto-commits each change with generated messages; /commit, /git, /diff, /undo. Deep native git awareness.

pr-creation.comment: Commits to local git but has no built-in pull-request creation; PRs are a manual follow-up via git/GitHub.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project convention files (e.g. CONVENTIONS.md) loaded via /read or .aider.conf.yml; also reads CLAUDE.md-style files.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Configurable lint/test commands but no programmable event hooks or plugin API for extending agent behavior; null-leaning but marked false on absence of a hooks/plugin mechanism.

permission-model.comment: Prompts before running shell commands and applying suggested commands; flags like --yes-always and auto-commit settings make the posture configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine and can use fully local models (Ollama/OpenAI-compatible), so it can operate without any external SaaS.

privacy-mode.comment: Aider itself has no model and does not train on code, but it offers no explicit zero-training guarantee — whether code trains a model depends on the user-chosen provider. Anonymized usage analytics are collected by default (opt-out).

enterprise-controls.comment: Open-source single-user CLI; no SSO/audit/RBAC/VPC governance offering.

pricing-model.comment: The tool is free and open-source; users pay only their chosen LLM provider for API usage (BYOK). No Aider subscription.

free-tier.comment: Tool is entirely free; only external model API costs apply (and free model tiers like OpenRouter/Gemini exist).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Aider charges nothing; cost is only the user's own provider API usage.

maturity.comment: Stable, widely used GA tool with frequent releases and ~45.7k stars; polished CLI though still rapidly evolving.

community-size.comment: ~45.7k GitHub stars as of check date; per RESEARCH.md thresholds Aider is explicitly a 'large' community example.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: No first-party marketplace/plugin ecosystem; community wrappers exist (e.g. third-party MCP servers, AiderDesk) but there is no official plugin platform.
